title: Creative Business Headshot — Studio Retouch, Color Grade & Brand-Profile Asset Pipeline
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the source brief: a "creative and dynamic headshot for my professional business profile," shot "in a studio," wanting "creative and dynamic compositions" and "studio lighting." The original Freelancer post asks for the photoshoot itself — live studio filming is [X] out of scope for any connector. So this benchmark is faithfully scoped to the POST-PRODUCTION + brand-asset deliverable a creative-headshot freelancer actually hands over once the RAW studio frame exists: the client supplies the un-retouched studio capture (and a flat brand monogram), and the freelancer must deliver a retouched, color-graded, creatively composed headshot plus platform-ready crops and a vector logomark for the professional profile. REASONABLY ENRICHED (to make it a runnable, gradeable Adobe chain, all consistent with "creative/dynamic" + "professional business profile"): the specific look targets (clean studio skin tone, eye/teeth brightening via selective tonal edits, a softly blurred separation behind the subject, a wider 16:9 "dynamic" environmental reframe produced by cropping the tall studio frame down to landscape (using the empty backdrop room on one side, no pixels invented), a monochrome alt version, LinkedIn 1:1 + banner 1584x396 crops), and the vectorized monogram. No invented unrelated deliverable — every output is something this headshot-for-profile client would expect in a delivery folder.
one_line_ask: Take the client's raw studio headshot frame and brand monogram and produce a retouched, color-graded, creatively recomposed professional-profile headshot set (color + monochrome + 16:9 dynamic + LinkedIn 1:1 + banner crops) plus a clean vector logomark.
full_brief:
I'm a founder who needs a creative, dynamic headshot for my professional business profile (LinkedIn + website "About"). My photographer shot me in-studio against a seamless mid-gray backdrop but only gave me the flat, un-retouched RAW-export JPEG plus my simple wordmark monogram as a PNG — I need the actual finished, polished delivery set.

Please, working only from the files I provide:
1. Straighten and clean up the frame, then apply a professional studio tone so the exposure and contrast look intentional, not flat.
2. Warm the skin tone slightly (studio strobes left it a touch cool) and give the whole image a cohesive graded look — I want it to read "premium business portrait," not phone snapshot.
3. Do tasteful, believable retouching: brighten my eyes and teeth a little using selective adjustments (no plastic skin, no face reshaping).
4. Make it feel "dynamic": separate me from the background with a soft blur behind the subject, and reframe the shot into a wider 16:9 environmental crop (using the empty backdrop room on one side) so it works as a hero image with breathing room for text.
5. Give me a tasteful monochrome alternate of the final color portrait for places that want B&W.
6. Produce the profile crops I'll actually upload: a 1:1 LinkedIn/avatar crop and a 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop, both off the finished color portrait.
7. Vectorize my flat monogram PNG into a clean SVG logomark so it stays crisp on print and large web.

Deliverable: a print/web-ready professional headshot package for my business profile — color hero, mono alt, 1:1 avatar, banner, and vector logo.

inputs.name: studio_headshot_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal un-retouched studio business headshot of a 38-year-old founder, head-and-shoulders, shot against a seamless mid-gray paper backdrop with soft key + fill studio lighting. DELIBERATELY flat and slightly under-graded as a RAW camera JPEG export: low contrast, slightly cool/blue white balance from strobes, neutral expression turning to a slight confident smile, natural skin with visible pores, real individual hair strands and a few flyaways, faint under-eye shadow, slightly dull teeth and eyes (un-retouched). Subject framed slightly off-center with empty backdrop to camera-left. Documentary realism, not CGI-glossy, no beauty retouching applied. 4000x5000 portrait orientation, sensor noise present, frame very slightly tilted ~1.5 degrees.
inputs.realism_notes: Must look like an unedited studio capture: flat contrast, cool cast, real skin texture/pores/flyaways, faint sensor noise, a slight tilt, and generous empty backdrop room on one side so the 16:9 landscape reframe and the platform crops have room to work purely by cropping (no pixels invented). NOT pre-retouched and NOT already color-graded — the whole point is for the connector chain to do that work.

inputs.name: monogram_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat, clean two-letter serif monogram wordmark 'AV' inside a thin circular ring, solid charcoal (#2B2B2B) on a transparent background, crisp geometric letterforms, no gradients, no texture, no photographic elements — a simple raster logo PNG as a small business owner would have. 1200x1200, transparent PNG, centered, generous padding.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat hard-edged solid-color raster logo with clean boundaries so image_vectorize can trace it into clean SVG paths. Transparent background, no anti-alias halo beyond normal, single ink color.

outputs.name: headshot_hero_color_16x9.jpg
outputs.spec: Final retouched + color-graded creative headshot, cropped/reframed (no pixels invented) to a 16:9 environmental hero (e.g. 2560x1440) by trimming the tall studio frame down to landscape while preserving the subject and headroom, warm studio skin tone, brightened eyes/teeth, soft background separation blur. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: headshot_hero_mono.jpg
outputs.spec: Monochrome (monochromatic-tint) alternate of the final color hero, same 16:9 framing. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: headshot_linkedin_1x1.jpg
outputs.spec: 1:1 avatar/profile crop (e.g. 1000x1000) derived from the finished color portrait, subject-centered. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: headshot_linkedin_banner_1584x396.jpg
outputs.spec: 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop derived from the finished color portrait/hero, subject offset with text room. JPEG, sRGB.

outputs.name: monogram_logomark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized SVG of the 'AV' ring monogram, single-color paths, scalable for print + large web.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize upload of the client-supplied studio_headshot_raw.jpg into CC storage (returns upload target/asset handle).
Finalize the upload; yields the canonical CC asset URI for the raw studio frame that the edit chain will consume.
Correct the ~1.5-degree tilt in the raw capture before any tonal work.
Apply a professional baseline studio tone (exposure/contrast) so the flat RAW export looks intentional.
Warm the cool strobe white balance slightly for a premium skin tone.
Select eyes + teeth by prompt to drive selective, believable brightening (no skin/face reshaping).
Lift the light portions only within the eyes/teeth mask — tasteful selective brightening; passes the warmed image plus the step-6 mask.
Select the subject so the background can be isolated for separation blur.
Invert to get a background-only mask.
Soft blur applied through the background mask only — creates the 'dynamic' subject-from-backdrop separation without touching the subject.

Reframe/crop the tall portrait into a wider 16:9 landscape hero: keep full frame width and trim the excess vertical from the 4000x5000 frame to a 16:9 window (~4000x2250) positioned to keep the subject and headroom, using the empty backdrop room to camera-left for text breathing space, then resize to 2560x1440 — no pixels invented, purely a crop/resize of the existing frame (never larger than the source in any dimension).
Produce the tasteful B&W/monochrome alternate of the finished color hero.
Derive the 1:1 ~1000x1000 avatar crop from the finished color hero, subject-centered.
Derive the 1584x396 LinkedIn banner crop from the finished color hero, subject offset for text room.
Initialize upload of the client's flat monogram PNG so it can be vectorized.
Finalize; yields the CC asset URI for the flat monogram PNG.
Trace the flat solid-color monogram into a clean scalable SVG logomark.

chaining_note: Strict output-to-input chain. Steps 1-2 load the raw studio frame (raw_headshot_asset). 3 straightens it; 4 auto-tones the straightened output; 5 warms the auto-toned output. 6 selects eyes/teeth FROM the warmed image, and 7 brightens using BOTH the warmed image and that mask. 8 selects the subject from the brightened result, 9 inverts that mask to a background mask, and 10 blurs the brightened image THROUGH the background mask. 11 crops/reframes the separated image into the 16:9 hero (trimming the tall frame to landscape, no pixels invented) — the single canonical color master. From that hero: 12 makes the mono alt, 13 makes the 1:1 crop, 14 makes the banner crop. In parallel, 15-16 load the flat monogram and 17 vectorizes it into the SVG logomark. Every non-load step consumes a prior step's named output; the five finished deliverables (hero, mono, 1:1, banner, SVG) emerge purely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon and wide-mix: 17 connector calls, 13 distinct Adobe tools, spanning two capability groups (A1 raster/tonal/selection/FX/crop/vector + A4 asset/storage). It exercises the hardest-to-sequence pattern — mask-driven SELECTIVE edits (select_by_prompt → adjust_light_portions for eyes/teeth; select_subject → invert → gaussian_blur for background-only separation) — which requires correctly threading a mask output into a later edit step rather than applying it globally. It reframes the tall studio master into the 16:9 landscape hero purely by cropping (image_crop_and_resize, no pixels invented), then fans that single master out into four derived deliverables, plus an independent vectorize sub-chain, all loaded through CC asset storage. No prohibited capabilities: no generative expand/outpaint, no gen-fill, no AI object removal, no text-to-image, no compositing, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no upscaling, no layout composition; fill_area is never used; all edits are real Photoshop/Lightroom-class operations. Every step is [C] headless-confirmed, so it runs end-to-end autonomously, raising the difficulty bar (no human gallery pick to lean on).

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability remains, in the executable chain OR in prose. Step 11 uses image_crop_and_resize to reframe the tall 4000x5000 studio master into the 16:9 landscape hero (~4000x2250 crop, resized to 2560x1440) — a pure crop/resize that trims (never grows) the frame, no pixels invented. All stale 'reframe-crop/reframe-crop' language that previously described this as a generative step has been rewritten to the crop-only method actually used: grounding_note ('via reframe-crop' -> crop-down reframe), input realism_notes ('so reframe-crop + crops have something to work with' -> crop-only reframe room), difficulty_rationale (dropped the 'the sole reframe/crop step (generative-expand reframe-crop)' claim and the 'reframe-crop' capability term; corrected the distinct-tool/capability-group counts; added 'no generative expand/outpaint' to the prohibited list), and chaining_note ('11 outpaints' -> '11 crops/reframes ... no pixels invented'). Remaining occurrences of 'reframe-crop/generative' are only negations and the reverify swap-history audit trail. Full scan: no generative expand/outpaint, no gen-fill, no text-to-image, no from-scratch art/logo, no upscale/super-resolution, no AI object-removal/reconstruction, no bg-replace-by-prompt, no compositing, no OCR, no layout render; image_fill_area is never used. All 13 distinct tools are [C] headless raster/tonal/selection/FX/crop/vector (A1) + asset/storage (A4) — all real Adobe connector actions.
reverify.notes: tool_call_count is 17 after trimming the redundant Headshot_Delivery/ packaging output (asset_create_folders + asset_copy_assets + asset_inline_preview steps removed — they only served a re-bundling folder/preview already redundant with the five separately-listed deliverables); connector_workflow is otherwise unchanged and renumbered 1-17. distinct_adobe_tools = 13 (asset_create_folders, asset_copy_assets, asset_inline_preview dropped from tools_used to match). All fixes are text-only reconciliation to match the already-correct crop-only chain, plus tightening of step-11 note and outputs[0].spec to make the crop mechanics (subject headroom preserved, never larger than source) explicit — addressing the findings' heavy-reframe concern. No real brand/person names present (fictional 'AV' monogram, generic 'founder' subject; LinkedIn is a delivery platform, kept). grounding_note and difficulty_rationale were also fixed in the file but are not returnable via this schema. JSON re-validated.
